instruction:
Given $ x=acos t, y=bsin t $ ($t$ is a parameter), then $frac{{{text{d}}^{2}}y}{text{d}{{x}^{2}}}=$( ).

Options:
A. $frac{b}{a}cs{{c}^{2}}t$
B. -frac{b}{{{a}^{2}}}cs{{c}^{2}}tcdot tan t
C. $-frac{b}{{{a}^{2}}}cs{{c}^{3}}tcdot tan t$
D. $-acs{{c}^{2}}tcdot sin t$
E. $frac{b}{{{a}^{2}}}cs{{c}^{2}}tcdot cos t$
F. $-frac{b}{{{a}^{2}}}cs{{c}^{2}}tcdot sin t$
G. $-frac{b}{{{a}^{2}}}cs{{c}^{2}}tcdot cos t$
H. $-frac{b}{{{a}^{2}}}cs{{c}^{3}}tcdot cos t$
I. $-frac{b}{{{a}^{2}}}cs{{c}^{3}}t$
J. $-frac{b}{{{a}^{2}}}cs{{c}^{3}}tcdot sin t$
